title: the surge of populism and the polarization of the society
content: The concept of populismTo begin with this topic, I want to introduce the concept of populism first. The notion, populism, was originally used to refer to the ideas and beliefs of the US People’s Party, which was founded in 1892. Later, the term was turned to be interpreted as mass manipulation. Although there is a lack of scholarly agreement about the nature of populism - is it a phenomenon, an ideology, or something else - , populism shares one significance feature that divided the society into two parts: the “pure” people and the “corrupt” elite, whatever it is right-populism or left-populism. In the history, the earliest populists are the Jacobins in the French Revolution, led by Robespierre. They preached the citizens who discontented about the Constitutional Monarchy because the assembly failed to meet their expectation and their incapability to control the inflation made people disappointed. Under these circumstances, the Jacobins utilized the trust from citizens to deceive sans-culottes to involve in the violence events against the Constitutional Monarchy. In this way, the Jacobins seized the political power and overthrown the Monarchy, but at the same time, the violence events are out of the control and led to the Regin of Terror where ten thousands of people were executed. Even in the modern society, the populism politicians are still renowned as preachers that trickily connect the discontents of average citizens and the dominance of elites in commercial, political, and advertising spheres. Take Donald Trump as a example, although he is an ambitious ex-president with personal charm to seize the public support, he utilities the support from citizens to strengthen the personal cult about himself. Consequently, when he failed to the 2020 presidency election, the most loyal voters of him denied the validity of the election and believed the conspiracy that Joe Biden, the winner of the election, and his Democrats fellows cheated in the election, preached by Donald Trump himself and influencers on the internet. With the anger caused by the “unfair” electoral result, the aggressive groups of Trump’s supporters attacked to the Capitol, the embodiment of the authority of the United States, and requested to re-elect. By observing the dramatic campaign, two questions emerged from my brain: why the conspiracy without any evidence can deceive the masses to a great extent and even indirectly result to the bleed event that we unexpected, and should the government limit the free speech on the Internet to avoid the widespread of misinformation and even conspiracy.
tags: populism 民粹; polarization 两极化; voting system 选举系统

title: Hello World
content: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info.
